popularStory.headline: Notes for Hugh Sargent and Margaret Gifford
popularStory.description:
Notes for Hugh Sargent:
Hugh Sargent must have been born about the year 1530. He died February 23, 1595/6 (buried the 1st of March). Unverified information suggests that Hugh's father was "John Sargen …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh Roger Sargent於1530年5月8日出生在英國時，他的父親Roger Maxwell Sargent25歲，母親Elizabeth Tale25歲。他於1554年2月23日與Agnes Margaret Gifford在英國諾薩普頓史爾庫廷哈爾結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1596年2月28日在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾庫廷哈爾享年65歲，埋葬在英國諾薩普頓史爾諾薩普頓哥吉拉 沙也 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hugh Roger Sargent于1530年5月8日出生在英国时，他的父亲Roger Maxwell Sargent25岁，母亲Elizabeth Tale25岁。他于1554年2月23日与Agnes Margaret Gifford在英国諾薩普頓史爾庫廷哈爾结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1596年2月28日在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾庫廷哈爾享年65岁，埋葬在英国諾薩普頓史爾諾薩普頓哥吉拉 沙也 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Sargent
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English sergeaunt ‘servant’ (Old French sergent, serjant). The surname probably originated for the most part in this sense, but the word also developed a number of more specialized meanings in the medieval period, including ‘tenant by military service below the rank of knight’. It was also used for a variety of administrative and legal offices, which may have contributed to the development of the surname. The sense ‘non-commissioned officer’ did not arise until the 16th century. Compare Sergent .

History: William Sargent (1624–1717) came to Gloucester, MA, from Devon, England before 1678. Many of his descendants distinguished themselves in the civil and military affairs of the colonies and some in literary or artistic paths, notably the portrait painter John Singer Sargent (1856–1925).

variants.name: Sergent
meanings: French: status or occupational name from Old French sergent, serjant ‘servant’ (see Sargent ).
Americanized form of Jewish (from Poland) Sierżant: artificial name meaning ‘sergeant’ (senior noncommissioned officer in the Polish infantry).

variants.name: Surgent
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Anatole, Emile.
Slovak: probably a nickname derived from Latin surgens, the present participle of surgo ‘to rise, to get up, to arise’.
French: probably a derivative of Surge, an occupational name from Old French surge ‘surgeon’.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh Roger Sargent was born on 8 May 1530, in England, his father, Roger Maxwell Sargent, was 25 and his mother, Elizabeth Tale, was 25. He married Agnes Margaret Gifford on 23 February 1554, in Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 28 February 1596, in Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65, and was buried in All Saints' Church, Northampton, Northamptonshire, England.

Il a épousé Agnes Margaret Gifford le 23 février 1554, à Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 28 février 1596, à Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à All Saints' Church, Northampton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh Roger Sargentが1530年5月8日にイギリスで生まれた時，父Roger Maxwell Sargentは25歳，母Elizabeth Taleは25歳であった。1554年2月23日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアコウルティーンハルでAgnes Margaret Giffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1596年2月28日にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアコウルティーンハルで65歳で死去し，イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアノーサンプトンアル サイントス' チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.All Saints' Church, Northampton, Northam: 영국 노르섬프토실르 노르섬프턴 앨 세인츠' c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Roger Sargent(이)가 1530년 5월 8일 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger Maxwell Sargent(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Tale(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1554년 2월 23일 영국 노르섬프토실르 쿠르티홀에서 Agnes Margaret Gifford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1596년 2월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 쿠르티홀에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르섬프토실르 노르섬프턴 앨 세인츠' c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh Roger Sargent nasceu em 8 de maio de 1530, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger Maxwell Sargent, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Tale, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Margaret Gifford em 23 de fevereiro de 1554, em Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 28 de fevereiro de 1596, em Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em All Saints' Church, Northampton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.

ru.All Saints' Church, Northampton, Northam: алл сеинц' чурч, нортамптон, нортамптоншир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh Roger Sargent родился 8 мая 1530 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Roger Maxwell Sargent было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Tale, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Margaret Gifford 23 февраля 1554 г. в местности коуртинолл, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он умер 28 февраля 1596 г. в местности коуртинолл, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности алл сеинц' чурч, нортамптон, нортамптоншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hugh Roger Sargent nació el 8 de mayo de 1530, en Inglaterra, su papá, Roger Maxwell Sargent, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Tale, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Agnes Margaret Gifford el 23 de febrero de 1554, en Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 28 de febrero de 1596, en Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en All Saints' Church, Northampton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.
